site.name: AI Engineering Portfolio
site.tagline: Graph ML, GNN explanation, GraphRAG, and applied AI engineering.
site.one_liner: GNN, GraphRAG, explainable AI, and production-minded AI engineering projects.

projects.title: RL-based GNN Counterfactual Explanation
projects.subtitle: Counterfactual explanation framework for graph neural networks.
projects.description: A research-oriented prototype that uses reinforcement learning to search graph edits that change a GNN prediction while preserving graph plausibility.
highlights: Defined graph edit actions for counterfactual search.
Separated explanation objective from downstream GNN architecture.
Tracked validity, sparsity, and prediction flip quality.

projects.title: NewTrend
projects.subtitle: Trend discovery and ranking system.
projects.description: An AI-assisted trend monitoring pipeline that collects signals, ranks topics, and summarizes emerging changes for decision support.
highlights: Built an end-to-end trend ranking workflow.
Combined structured metrics with generated summaries.
Designed outputs for repeated operational use.

projects.title: GraphRAG / Knowledge Graph Prototype
projects.subtitle: Retrieval-augmented generation with graph-structured context.
projects.description: A prototype that organizes entities and relations into a knowledge graph and uses graph-aware retrieval for more grounded generation.
highlights: Modeled entities and relations as explicit graph context.
Compared graph retrieval with plain chunk retrieval.
Focused on traceability and inspectable context.

projects.title: FinExplain
projects.subtitle: Explainable finance-oriented AI prototype.
projects.description: A finance explanation prototype that converts model outputs and market signals into transparent, reviewable explanations.
highlights: Prioritized interpretability over opaque score reporting.
Structured explanations around evidence and uncertainty.

research.title: Master's Research Summary
research.summary: Research focused on graph-based machine learning and explainable AI. Replace this text with the final thesis title and abstract.
research.problem_definition: Many GNN predictions are difficult to interpret, especially when users need actionable explanations rather than only feature attribution.
research.proposed_method: The proposed approach explores counterfactual graph edits or graph-structured reasoning to explain model behavior in a more actionable way.
research.experimental_results: Summarize datasets, baselines, metrics, and the strongest quantitative or qualitative findings here.
research.limitations_and_extensions: Current limitations include scalability, domain-specific validity constraints, and evaluation difficulty. Future work can extend to larger graphs, stronger validity checks, and human-centered explanation studies.

blog.title: GNN Counterfactual Explanation Notes
blog.summary: A short note on why counterfactual explanations are useful for graph neural networks.
blog.content: Counterfactual explanations ask what minimal graph change would alter a model prediction. This framing can be more actionable than only highlighting important nodes or edges.

blog.title: GraphRAG and Knowledge Graph Retrieval
blog.summary: A short note on when graph-structured retrieval can help RAG systems.
blog.content: GraphRAG is useful when entity relations matter. Instead of retrieving isolated chunks, a system can retrieve connected facts and expose the reasoning context.

blog.title: AI Engineering Lessons from MVPs
blog.summary: A short note on building AI prototypes that survive real demos.
blog.content: A useful AI MVP needs fallback paths, visible status, reproducible outputs, and honest uncertainty. The engineering around the model often matters as much as the model itself.
